paper_assumptions_checked: AMS should iteratively refine sampling around 2D pose guided locations.
Out-of-range grid_sample coordinates should produce zero-valued features.
Depth and image features should be aligned to pose without stale forward state.
Forward calls should not mutate caller tensors or feature lists.

findings.evidence: mvn/models/DGLifting.py appends depth_images directly to features_list_hr.
findings.why_it_matters: A reused feature list silently keeps stale depth tokens. This can make repeated probes or ablations compare the wrong depth map.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Use features_list_hr = list(features_list_hr) + [depth_images], then add a regression test that list length stays unchanged after forward.

findings.issue: AMS uses border padding for refined sampling
findings.evidence: DGLifting.py DeformableBlock calls F.grid_sample(..., padding_mode='border', align_corners=True).
findings.why_it_matters: The paper says coordinates outside [-1, 1] yield zero-valued features. Border padding instead copies edge features, so large offsets can harvest border artifacts rather than being penalized.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Change refined AMS sampling to padding_mode='zeros' or explicitly justify border behavior with an out-of-bound sampling test.

findings.why_it_matters: The training loop often passes clone(), but the model contract itself is unsafe. Reusing the same tensor across calls double-normalizes coordinates and breaks grid_sample alignment.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Clone inside DGPose.forward before normalization and add a test that caller input is unchanged.

findings.why_it_matters: The paper reports Cfuse=128 and 4 AMS iterations, but hard-coding makes ablations and replacement modules harder to compare fairly across configs.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Read these values from config with paper defaults as fallbacks, then test a small non-default config can instantiate.

findings.issue: Depth log-variance is not clipped as described in the paper
findings.evidence: mvn/models/loss.py has no clamp for s before exp(-s).
findings.why_it_matters: Although this is tied to the auxiliary depth head, it affects training stability for any replacement depth module that still emits supervised depth.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Clamp s to [-9, 9] in the depth auxiliary loss and add an extreme-s test that loss stays finite.

findings.issue: Depth branch uses a shallow conv on scalar depth maps
findings.evidence: DGLifting.py maps [B,H,W] depth to Cfuse with one 3x3 Conv2d.
findings.why_it_matters: The paper denotes D as a depth feature representation. A single conv may under-represent local discontinuities that AMS is supposed to exploit.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Use a depth-edge synthetic probe: perturb a narrow limb-like depth discontinuity and verify depth tokens change more near affected joints than far joints.

findings.evidence: features_list length changed from 4 to 5 after one DGLifting.forward call.
findings.why_it_matters: A second call with the same list will contain old embedded depth and can ignore the new appended depth in parts of AMS.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Make the list append functional, then rerun this script and expect unchanged length.

findings.issue: Reusing feature lists can crash DGLifting.forward
findings.evidence: Second forward with the same feature list raised IndexError: index 5 is out of bounds for dimension 1 with size 5
findings.why_it_matters: This confirms the feature-list append is not just a measurement artifact; it can break repeated calls and any wrapper that caches/reuses backbone features.
findings.suggested_test_or_fix: Make DGLifting.forward copy the list before appending depth, then rerun this script and expect no exception.
